title: galium ferrugineum subsp. ferrugineum
intro: Galium ferrugineum subsp. ferrugineum, also known as rusty bedstraw, is a flowering plant species within the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Galium ferrugineum subsp. ferrugineum include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. The herbal preparation of the plant can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions in traditional medicine.
